game.title: Evaw
game.short_text: Become one with the waves to traverse this fragmented land

details.title: Evaw
details.description:
Evaw, the last survivor of the nation, explores the destroyed world around them. Use the nation's downfall to your advantage and traverse the eerie, empty world, perhaps finding some hope of another survivor. What's that? A signal from a radio speaking in poems?  The world has been gone for hundreds of years. Who could this signal be coming from? 
Feathers will impress your friends, but that's about it. Only collect them if you really want to!This game is not meant to be completed in one sitting. Take breaks. Get some water. Relax!
Controls:
KeyboardControllerMoveA/D,  Arrow KeysLeft joy-stick,  D-padJumpW,  Up Arrow,  SpaceA,  BLight waveRMB,  XRight TriggerSound waveLMB,  ZLeft TriggerInteractE,  CX,  YPauseEscape, P+,  StartRestartR-,  Back
Use light and sound waves to navigate 10 challenging yet beautiful levels. Find feathers along the way and uncover more about the shattered reality you inhabit. If you are having trouble or feel the game is too easy, head over to settings to adjust the difficulty. There is also a built-in speedrun timer, allowing you to race your friends and have fun! Our team's record for all feathers is 9:44, see if you can beat it!
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
"Multiple regions began moving at an incredibly fast speed, while others remained at an outer observable standstill. The smallest of these time fragments could be no bigger than a house, causing madness and incomprehensible fatalities at exorbitant levels. Neither the waves nor the inhabitants could work in harmony. From that, Evaw was born."Credits:
Programming:  James Glaser, Mateo Wright
Art: Gwyn Roy
VFX: Gwyn Roy
Music: Roman Reid Weatherdon
SFX: Roman Reid Weatherdon
Level design: Gwyn Roy, James Glaser, Mateo Wright

Made in ~2 weeks for Github Game Off 2025 Ooqo ranked 7th overall, 1st in Graphics and Gameplay over 700+ entries !

Moving on an empty tile cost life, moving on a fish don't

details.title: Froggy Love
details.description:
❤️ Froggy Love ❤️
⭐Update 
We’ve expanded our game to 7 levels to celebrate ranking 7th in GameOff 2025! 
Enjoy the new scenes and challenges.
 
🐸Game Overview
FroggyLove is a therapeutic 2D puzzle adventure game, rendered in a charming pixel art style and developed using the Unity engine. Players navigate a serene pond environment, aiming to reunite one froggy with its loved one.
The core mechanic involves using ripples to influence the froggy's movement across the water's surface. By strategically throwing stones, you create water waves that push the main character, bringing it closer to its goal.
The game features three distinct levels, each offering a unique scenario and increasing difficulty.
Are you ready to become Froggy's matchmaker?

🐸Controls
• Create a Water Ripple: Tap (Quick Click)
• Create a Larger Wave: Press and Hold, then Release
Tips & Notes
• You'll want to stay far away from the snakes!
• Always keep an eye on your stone count—they are a limited resource.

 
🐸Our Team
• Programmer: Bai
• Game Artist: Farrah U
Connect With Us
We welcome your comments and look forward to hearing your feedback!

game.title: La Ola
game.short_text: An endless-runner typing game!

details.title: La Ola
details.description:
La Ola: An Endless Runner Typing Game!
Type words as fast as you can and try to get a leaderboard high score!
Press the keys matching the letters on the signs held up by the crowd to move the Mexican wave - remember to press space between words when your reach a gap!
You may encounter sleeping people in the crowd - do your best and guess what letter they are holding, using the rest of the letters in the crowd!
Don't take too long typing, or you will lose!
How fast can you type? How long can you make the wave survive?
Made in 30 days for GitHub Game Off 2025: "WAVES" [Link to Submission Page]. The game placed 9th overall, and 1st place in the "Theme Interpretation" category :)
Credits: 
Production: Jay "Bliss" ArbuthnotArt: Evie Winter 
Programming: Conor Wilson, Sankha PeirisProgramming and Music: Emmet Winter
Special thanks to Marcus Galic, who was part of our brainstorming sessions :)
In case you were wondering: 
"La Ola" translates to "The Wave" in Spanish :)
The game generates the text using a Marcov Chain, which is similar to how predictive-text works on your phone. We adapted an old public-domain sports broadcast as our text source ⚾

game.title: The Last Wave
game.short_text: Solve the mysteries with Saja. (English & 한국어 & 日本語)

details.title: The Last Wave
details.description:
!Trigger warning: Fatal fire incident!
Note this:
If you're not Korean, this information will help you understand the game better:
 
 "Han (한)" refers to a complex emotion involving sorrow, resentment, and a sense of resignation—yet not quite giving up entirely.
 In Korea, the family name comes first, followed by the given name.
 "Saja (사자)" in Korean can mean both a deceased soul and a messenger (from the afterlife).

Introduction
Solve mysteries and resolve "han" with the Saja (Korea's grim reaper).
A midnight blaze consumed the building, claiming ten lives in the ashes.
The culprit has been caught. The case is closed.
 
...But your work is only just beginning.
 
"Hmph, not bad for a rookie. ...What? 'Can't even do this?' You little...!"
Together with your prickly yet endearingly clumsy Saja partner, you must uncover the identities of the deceased and help them find peace.
 
Who were they? What keeps them bound to this world?
 
In a building filled with half-burned belongings and fading memories, uncover their  stories.
About
This game was created for Game-Off 2025 Game Jam, where the theme was WAVES.
This game is a mystery inspired heavily by "Return of the Obra Dinn", with elements of point-and-click adventure and visual novel storytelling.
The playtime is approximately 20 minutes.
The game's most appealing features are:
1. Your quirky and endearing Saja (Korea's grim reaper) partner
2. Deduction mechanics where you must match the wave, name, and location to restore each soul's identity
3. An emotional story
4. A meticulously crafted ending sequence
I'd be truly grateful if you play through to the very end!
Credit
adaid & gasbank developed this game!
Full Credit

moreInfo.Tags: Detective, Mystery, Pixel Art, Point & Click, saja

game.title: Solais
game.short_text: Keep your family safe and the lighthouse operational as you play as Sutherland, a lighthouse keeper in Scotland.

details.title: Solais
details.description:
Optimized for desktop (due to the high graphics and audio requirements). However, you can play up until the end credits in browser. Thank you for playing our game!
Solais
Step into the unforgiving landscape of WWII Scotland as a Scottish Lighthouse Keeper. Your duty is not just to maintain the light, but to guard your family against German attacks and the encroaching horrors of war.
Solais is a narrative-driven game with multiple endings, taking its dramatic foundation from the tragic real-life events at the Fair Isle Lighthouse. Balance survival with duty through five engaging minigames and an immersive atmosphere.
We pride ourselves on originality: All art, music, voice overs, writing, and programming is Gen-AI free and created by our team. In addition, no premade assets were used in this game.

Gameplay
Controls
On your first day in Solais, you will be guided through new mechanics in tutorial mode .
Movement: Navigate the lighthouse interior using standard WASD or the Arrow Keys.Interaction: Most object interaction is handled with the E Key and R Key.Navigation: Press M to view the Map of the lighthouse, or select the corresponding icon on screen.Task Management: Check your tasks with the Q Key or by selecting the task menu to review your current objectives.Dialogue: Advance conversation with any input (e.g., Spacebar, Enter, or Mouse Click).Minigames: All five core minigames are designed for intuitive engagement using simple click and/or drag mechanics. Every new mechanic is accompanied by a clear, non-intrusive guide.
Note: Save functionality only works if downloaded, not in browser.
Storyline
Solais's storyline takes place over the course of 5 in-game days. Through dialogue and decisions, you can obtain one of two different endings. Remember, choose carefully. The fate of your family is in your hands.


GitHub GameOff
This game was made in one month as a submission for the 2025 GitHub GameOff.
Theme Interpretation
Our team was excited to hear the theme of "waves" announced. We embraced the interpretation of the literal waves of the ocean by setting the scene on the harsh Scottish coastline. However, we managed to incorporate the theme in a couple of other ways as well. The title of our game, Solais, is Scottish Gaelic for "light." And while the quantum physics enthusiast on our team points out that light has a wave-partical duality, the concept of light waves was central to our design. Lastly, as a bonus, one of our minigames is very wave-oriented, as you'll find out (we won't give away the fun yet)!
Our Team
Our main team was composed of 7 very talented individuals. We had 1 project manager, 1 writer, 2 programmers, 2 artists, and 1 composer. Though, like with most indie games, everyone involved wore multiple hats. We also had the pleasure of working with 3 voice actors and 1 independent musician.
GitHub Repository: https://github.com/charlievit/Nyxterra

game.title: WAVE / LENGTH
game.short_text: A game about the waves we make.

details.title: WAVE / LENGTH
details.description:
WAVE / LENGTH
A game about the waves we make.
About
Wave/Length follows the story of Harry, a lonely tropical island
radio operator, who encounters a series of paranormal entities trying to
break through into our universe. 
Players must help Harry uncover the true power of the wave in order to protect his universe from a catastrophic paradoxical reality collapse.
Playable + full
download available @ itch.io
Full source code
available on GitHub
This project is my very first attempt at:
Creating a video game (at
least since QBasic as a kid) Making my own art assets Joining a game
jam
Built using Godot 4.1 stable, for the 2025 GitHub Game Off
#GitHubGameOff.
Controls
Controls:
WASD - Move
E - Interact
Mouse wheel - Tune radio emitter (Hold shift to fine tune)
Left Mouse Button - Emit a radio burst
There are two alternative ways to tune the radio, depending on your
hardware:
Hold Q + move mouse left and right - Fine tune radio emitter
1..7 - Jump to station (use -/= to fine tune)
AI disclaimer:
Everything in the game and source code repository - all code and
artwork - is my own handmade work, except for the music and font assets
mentioned in the Credits section below, and a backdrop image created by
my wife.
However I've marked this project as including AI assets because I
leaned on ChatGPT heavily for: 
Learning Godot (Minor code snippets / I
asked ChatGPT lots of questions about how to do things in Godot whilst
learning) Concept art / sprite ideas (I copied the look and feel of
some generative AI picture ideas when creating my own novice pixel art) ChatGPT generated me a Krita vapor wave color palette to keep my colors
nice and consistent. 
Licenses and Attributions
Please see the LICENSE file in the source repo for the
exact license covering the bulk of this project. This license applies to
all original work created by me for this game (Godot code, Krita
artwork, supplementary files, etc.).
Third-party assets (music, fonts, sound effects, etc.) are
not covered by the main project license and remain
under their original licenses, as listed below in the Credits
section.

Credits
With great love and respect I'd like to thank the following creators
whose artwork was included in this project <3
Pizza Doggy (itch.io)
Thanks to Pizza Doggy for their awesome Cozy Tunes pack - and
extra thanks for giving me special permission to upload their amazing
work to an open source repository.
The .ogg files in
music/pizzadoggy.itch.io/* are covered by their
Game Asset License Agreement, NOT the license applicable to
the rest of the project.
Please see the README.md file in that folder for further
information.

Thanks to Zeeey for the amazing track "The Litch". This
track was not listed with a license, but Zeeey granted me explicit
permission to use their banging track in this game and to include it in
this repository.
The file
music/officialzeeey.itch.io/TheEnigmaFinished.mp3 (track
name "The Litch") remains the sole intellectual property of Zeeey and is
NOT covered by the main project license. It may not be reused,
redistributed, or incorporated into other projects without obtaining
permission directly from Zeeey.
alkakrab (itch.io)

game.title: Mecha Resonance
game.short_text: A space-based shoot-em-up where you navigate through wave-distorted Frequency Domains. Return the Core, restore harmony.

details.title: Mecha Resonance
details.description:
About:
Mecha Resonance is a space-based shoot-em-up where you navigate through wave-distorted Frequency Domains, fighting corrupted resonance patterns and unstable VOX prototypes.
Fight through waves of oncoming enemy space crafts and upgrade your stats to prepare for your fight against The Conductor! Make sure to use your two color shields to block their oncoming blasts and FIRE a powerful wave back at them at full power!
---
Centuries after an M.R.C. facility fell quiet, its prototype units, the VOXES, awaken corrupted and unstable.

You are RES-08, carrying the Sound Core into the heart of the ruined station.
Trace, defend, and counterwave through fighting and shifting enemy signals as you push toward the facility’s core.
Your goal is simple: reach the Conductor and return the Sound Core.
Restore harmony.  Restore the resonance.
---
UPDATES:Version 1.1 was uploaded 20 minutes past submission.  In the first submitted build the currency system and some sound effects were broken.
---
Controls:
[ P ] - Pause
[ Left Shift ] - Dash
[ Space ] - Fire Shot
[ Left Click ] - Green Shield
[ Right Click ] - Blue Shield
[ F ] - Super Wave (Only when gauge is full from blocking correct shield blasts)
Credits:
Matt Chandler - Game Design Programmer, Audio Implementation 
Alex Norfleet (NanoComputer) - Game and UI Programmer / Designer 
Áiron Bruce (Estellairon) - AI Programmer, VFX Design
 Zachary Adams (VolkerA4) - Game Design, Programming 
Alex sovereign - Character Design 
Abdullah M (Laeirok) - 3D Artist 
Paleta Rosa - Story, Script, Game Logo 
2K - Main Composer 
FOUDIA - Supporting Composer 
Special Thanks: 
Juka - Sound Implementation 
Jade - Art Concepts
Download the .zip file and extract into its own folder. Then run the GameOff2025.exe to begin the game!

game.short_text: This is a fishing game similar to the gameplay of Gold Miner

details.title: Echo Fisher
details.description:
Made for [Game Off 2025]
This is a game inspired by Gold Miner, but trust me, the experience is truly unique— Because you can't "see" a thing.

Use your radar to detect the fish, predict their trajectory, and strike with precision. The game features upgrades, a combo system, relics, a shop, and even 3 beautiful unlockable illustrations!

Trust me, it's a blast to play. And most importantly... the cat is adorable, isn't it?

Note: Once you unlock [Aiming], please hold 'W' to aim and release to fire. Please forgive me for not having enough time to create a proper tutorial for this mechanic!

game.title: Waveforge
game.short_text: Physics Puzzle Game Made for Github Game Off 2025

details.title: Waveforge
details.description:
Waveforge is a 2D physics-based puzzle game where players manipulate water waves and emergent physical phenomena to guide a rubber duck to the goal. Set within a meticulously crafted pixel-art physics world reminiscent of Noita, it invites players to experience uniquely engaging physical interactions. Featuring over ten thoughtfully designed levels, the game delivers about 1 hour of delightfully challenging puzzle-solving gameplay.
Controls

In Levels:

    Left Click: Place selected tool
    Mouse Wheel: Adjust item brush size
    W / Up Arrow: Previous item
    S / Down Arrow: Next item
    1-9: Direct item selection
    R (Double-tap): Retry level
    ESC: Pause game

In Menus (Keyboard-Only Navigation)

    WASD / Arrow Keys: Navigate UI
    Space / Enter: Confirm selection
    ESC: Go back

Pro Tip: Play with sound enabled! Audio cues make animations feel responsive. (Can skip animations in Settings if preferred)
FAQ
Where can I find the source code for this project?
The full source code is available on GitHub. You can find it here: https://github.com/szdytom/waveforge. Please consider giving us a star if you enjoyed the game.
I get an error about a missing msvcrt.dll (or similar). How do I fix this?
This error means your system is missing the required Microsoft Visual C++ runtime libraries, which the game needs to run.
Most Windows computers already have this installed. If yours doesn't, you can download the latest installer directly from Microsoft.
Solution: Please download and install the latest "v14" (for Visual Studio 2017–2026) version from this official page: Latest Supported Visual C++ Redistributable.
On Linux, I get an error like "libxrandr.so.2 not found". What should I do?
Creating a single executable that works on all Linux distributions is challenging due to varying system libraries. This error indicates a missing dependency.
Here are your best options to resolve this:

    
        Install the Required Libraries: The most straightforward solution is to install the missing package. The command varies by distribution:
        
            On Ubuntu/Debian: sudo apt install libxrandr2 libgl1-mesa-dev libxi-dev libxcursor-dev
            On Arch Linux: sudo pacman -S libxrandr lib32-libxrandr
            On Fedora: sudo dnf install libXrandr mesa-libGL-devel libXi-devel libXcursor-devel
        
    
    
        Build from Source (Recommended for Advanced Users): If the above doesn't work, the most reliable method is to compile the game on your system. This ensures all dependencies are met.
        
            Clone the repository: git clone https://github.com/szdytom/waveforge
            Follow the build instructions in the project's README.md file.
        
    
    
        Use the Windows Version with Wine: As an alternative, you can download the Windows release and run it using Wine, this is tested to work well.
    

I'm stuck on the main menu. I clicked "Start" but nothing happens!
The game's menu is navigated using the keyboard, not the mouse. Please refer to the "Controls" section for more details. In general, you should

    Use W/A/S/D or the Arrow Keys to highlight menu options.
    Press Enter or Space to confirm your selection.

A specific level is too difficult for me. Can I skip ahead to try the next one?
We understand that some puzzles can be challenging! For the purposes of game jam evaluation and to ensure players are never permanently stuck, we've included a way to unlock all levels.
If you wish to proceed, you can unlock the entire game from the settings menu by typing xyzzy. This will give you access to all levels.
Additionally, if you're looking for hints or want to see how others have solved a puzzle, we encourage you to visit the community discussion board to share and compare solutions.
Team Members

    Code: fang_erj
    Music & SFX: stevvven
    Art: ZTL-UwU

Acknowledgements
We thank zurry for their inspiring thoughts on gameplay, mechanics, and level design. We would also like to thank RitaRossweisse301 for early testing and feedback.
We also thank these open source projects:

    microsoft/proxy: C++20 library for "Next Generation Polymorphism"
    SFML: Simple and Fast Multimedia Library
    cpptrace: C++ stack trace library
    Aseprite: Pixel art creation tool

game.title: Shorebound
game.short_text: Fend off swarms of enemies while surviving ocean waves on the beach!

details.title: Shorebound
details.description:
Shorebound
One fateful day, a lone crab gets swept away from his home by the raging ocean. When the tide recedes, the crab is left stranded in hostile territory. Help the crab fight his way home as you fend off waves of enemies, and burrow to avoid the waves of the ocean.

Controls
WSAD or Arrow Keys to move
SPACEBAR to burrow (when burrow meter is full)

How to play
Defeat enemies with your crab claw, or the ancient mystic crab arts that take the form of three legendary weapons. As you defeat enemies, they will drop shell shards. Collect these shards to fill your burrow meter and heal your crab. 
When you see the warning, beware! A wave is coming! Burrow at the last second to avoid being swept away. But be careful, as you can still take damage while burrowed. Make sure to time it right!
While you are burrowed under a wave, you will be given the option to select one of three upgrades. The longer you survive the more powerful you become!
Good luck! Making it to the end of this game is not for the faint of heart. Can you survive the deadly shores of Crab Coast?

details.title: Wave Warden
details.description:
WAVE WARDEN

    Your lighthouse is the last defense against the creatures of the night. Sea beasts pour in from the surf— Sturdy Crustaceans ,  Slithering Sea Serpents,  and Boulder-throwing Octopi!

  You will slow, blind, and stun them with precise cursor aim. 

 Level up, choose upgrades (bigger beam, harder hits, crits, XP boosts), and pop Overcharge or Lens Flare when things get hairy.  Survive as long as you can, chase new high scores, and try not to lose the last boat.
How long will you last?
This game is for the 2025 Game off Game jam. This is my first ever Game Jam and enjoyed every bit of this. I pushed myself as a solo dev and put (way too!) many hours into this. I did all the art/animation/fx and development. The music was done by my awesome cousin Adam! I hope its fun, and PLEASE LEAVE FEEDBACK in the comments!

Game Description
Play as an adorable fox with a pulse gun! Weave through hazards and push enemies to their deaths as you wander the abandoned alien spaceship you wake up in. 
Explore the ships level each with multiple hidden challenge sublevels. Make your way to the ship's reactor to stop it exploding. 
Collect ammo and other upgrades as you go. Gather runes on each level to reach 100% completion!
Undulo is a pixel platformer created in one month for the GitHub November Game Jam in 2025, the theme for the jam is 'Wave'.

Controls
Player Movement
Keyboard
A = move leftD = move right
Pulse/Air Movement
Mouse
Pulses you toward the mouse position
Arrow keys
Right Arrow = mid air pulse right (push pulse left)
Left Arrow = mid air pulse left (push pulse right)
Up Arrow = mid air pulse up (push pulse down)
Down Arrow= mid air pulse down (push pulse up)

Misc Controls
Space or mouse click = close dialogue box

Esc = pause/unpause

game.title: How to Exorcise Your Husband
game.short_text: Follow three old ladies in their quest. Armed with musical instruments, you will fight each husband by solving puzzles.

details.title: How to Exorcise Your Husband
details.description:
How to Exorcise Your Husband
Bernard, Josette's late husband, still very attached to the hold he has over his wife, cannot leave her alone, even in the afterlife. Desperate, she calls on her loyal friends, Halima and Dorothy. The three widows then embark together on a quest where each will have to face her past and exorcise their respective husbands.
"How to exorcise your husband" is a short puzzle game where you follow three old ladies in their impossible quest. You will have to face the ghosts that haunt each protagonist's past. Armed with special ghost-hunting musical instruments that they ordered online, you will have to fight each husband by solving a series of puzzles. 
Each level will require you to find the ghost's position in the grid. Trap it using the anti-ghost device. And finally, destroy its ghostly energy by positioning each protagonist at the right distance, so that the wave generated by their instrument resonates with the ghost's paranormal energy.
A complete run should take between 15 to 40 minutes. Your progress are saved in your browser cookies, so feel free to take a break if you need and to come back later!
We added a few settings that are also saved across play sessions. Don't hesitate to take a look at the settings menu to tweak your experience. Some of the visuals can be quite heavy on performances. You can also unlock all levels at once and skip dialogues if you starve for puzzles but are not interested in the narrative, we respect that.
Enjoy! And if you liked the game, feel free to share your experience with us and leave a comment!💌
How to playThe game can be played with mouse only. In order to solve a puzzle you have to find where the ghost is on screen, move the trap to its location, and finally move each characters at the correct distance from the trapping device.Be careful, you can only do 3 moves per turn, and at the end of each turn the ghost will moves to another grid cell, following a specific pattern. However, if the trap is on the same grid cell than the ghost at the end of the turn, it will cancel the ghost movement, which gives you some space to think about how to catch it for good!Your goal is to balance your actions between finding where the ghost is on the map and trapping it on location to get a chance to kill it before the end of the next turn.Hint: When selecting a character, a set of possible position for the ghost is displayed as a colored highlighted cell. By looking at where each character think the ghost is, you may be able to reduce the number of possibilities.Hint: The waves are shown as observed by the trapping device. This means that you may have aligned all waves properly but the paranormal resonance would be off if the ghost isn't on the same grid cell as the trap! Be sure to put the trap where you think the ghost is.Context
This game was created during the month of November 2025 as an entry for the Game Off 2025 game jam. The imposed theme was WAVES, which quickly drove us in the direction of the physical interpretation of waves as perturbations propagating through space and the different phenomena those generate.
We started by exploring the idea of simulating wave propagation in a 2d space and started to poke the concept around to see if we would be able to make a game from it.
After a few rounds of iteration and prototype testing, we finally froze our concept to the current puzzle game, where you have to position wave emitters in space such that the resulting wave measured by an observer match a given pattern. In order to make the game fun we had to leave behind most of the actual physics and mathematics of waves, extracting the core idea of phase shift depending on distance. 
The whole narrative emerged naturally from our concept and led to (at least we hope) something fun to play.
The game has been built using Godot 4.5 and the entire codebase is open source and available on Github.
About us
We are a team of four enthusiasts who love making games together: 
@a_sett, our favorite audio engineer who crafted all audio assets and wrote the dynamic music you will hear in game. Listen to more of his work on his itchio page.@Jivechameleon, our talented illustrator who designed all characters and hand-drown all illustrations. More from her on her itchio page.@LaChapeliere, our creative narrative designer. She imagined the backstory and added depth to each character. You can find more of her games on her itchio page.
@KinoxKlark, physicist, programmer, and game dev enthusiast. He put together the codebase of the project, gameplay interactions and animation. You can play more of his games on  his itchio page.
💜 We would like to also thanks our play testers who gave us very valuable feedbacks!💜
Credits
As much as we love crafting our games with care, we may not always be able to do everything ourselves. In addition to our original assets, we gathered a few free of charges assets on the web. Here are all the necessary credits, and our thanks for those creators.
Color palette: We used the amazing Mulfok32 Color Palette by @mulfokFont: Our titles use CAT Altenglisch font by @PeterWiegelFont: Most of our in-game texts use Roboto font by GoogleWe borrowed a few icons from Font Awesome 7 free set

game.title: Lia's Farm Stop
game.short_text: Lia revives a farm beside a bus stop, turning it into a cozy escape.

details.title: Lia's Farm Stop
details.description:
Welcome to Lia's Farm Stop! Unwind, enjoy the sights, try fresh produce, enjoy a place beyond the city. 

After inheriting her grandfather’s farm beside a quiet bus stop, Lia begins restoring the land to its former beauty. Travelers from the nearby town arrive seeking peace and fresh air, turning her humble farm into a cozy escape in the countryside. The waves of visitors get to enjoy a time away while Lia creates a sanctuary. 
A game created for Game Off 2025, using the theme waves. 

Using coins, help Lia rebuild her grandfather's farm. Calling the bus will have visitors arrive, where they will seek out nearby sights they wish to see, indicative by their thought bubbles. More sights = happier visitors. Happy visitors drop coins that allow Lia to continue fixing up the farmstead, creating more sights and activities for future visitors to enjoy. 
Accompanied by a wonderful soundscape and splashes of storybook art, the farm slowly comes to life, becoming a place where Lia can honour her grandfather's memory.
Controls  [A] and [D] - move left and right

[E] - interact/build

[B] - call bus (at bus stop) 
Credits HumaneTiger - 2d artwork, animation, coding 

Jane Wei - music, sound design, audio

Harley Brown - dialogue 
We hope you have enjoyed Lia's Farm Stop.

game.title: pale broadcast
game.short_text: A short, eerie game with a radio, ghost voices, and the end of the world.

details.title: pale broadcast
details.description:
pale broadcast is a short, surreal and a little creepy puzzle/walking sim, where your only tool is a radio receiver. Search the frequencies, decode messages, and take the steps given to you. Made in my after-work hours over 20 days for Game Off 2025!
Heavily inspired by real-world numbers stations and mystery radio frequencies. I wanted to shoot for that strange, unsettling feeling that stations like the Buzzer and EAS alarms give you- hopefully it worked out well lol
I've wanted to make a game with the radio system since I built it, and Game Off 2025's prompt (and the creative license the jam applies to it) gave me a perfect opportunity- hence I have gone for the option of radio waves! I haven't made anything particularly creepy or surreal before, nor put this much time into the sound design of one project, so this is a bit out of my normal zone- though I'm pretty happy with how it ended up :))))
Final note, I tried desperately for a week to get a web build working for this game, but unfortunately I just couldn't pull it off in the end- thanks WebGL's limited audio capabilities!!!!

Controls


Q and E to tune the radio, hold SHIFT to tune faster and CTRL to tune slower
H to show a hint
In the second part, WASD to move and LEFTCLICK to interact.
and ESC to show the volume options
The solution to the first puzzle is in the README of the GitHub repo if you get decidedly stuck !!

Credits


Audio, programming, art, design and all the other things by me (ryle-e) (Ryley) :))This project uses my open-source Unity package Ryle Radio, so you can try it yourself if you likeThank you to Mark, Rory, Ben, Jeht, Jaiden, and Emma for beta testing and feedback during development :)))))For the Windows builds, extract the zip file and run 
`pale broadcast.exe`
In the Mac builds, run the `.app` file in the zip
Make sure you check controls on the itch page first!

details.title: Waves From Beyond
details.description:
Survive the night...
You will play as Karmela - a young ranger on her first day at work - who will soon learn that the forest silence can be as terrifying as any scream.
The night shift begins peacefully. Calls from some campers - nothing special - until the phone rings again.

This one is a different call. A call of despair asking for aid. A serial killer roams the forest after escaping from prison and is after a new victim.

As a forest ranger, protecting its visitors is your duty. But as you guide them towards a safe haven you start hearing voices. Whispers that interfere with the radio frequency waves… the voices of the innocents ended by the killer’s blade.   

Some spirits tell the truth.

Some just want to drag you with them.

It depends on which one you want to believe.
Each decision might be the line between life and death both for the victim and you.  

Controls

LMBInteract
RMBReturn
PPause Game

game.title: Approve Please
game.short_text: Step into Release Ops. Review PRs, hold the line on stability, and try not to get fired

details.title: Approve Please
details.description:
A short browser game where you’re the overworked engineer whose whole job is reviewing GitHub pull requests. 
  Approve fast, catch bugs, don’t get fired. Available in English and Spanish.



  Step into Release Ops and survive a week on code review duty.
  
    From 9:00 to 17:00, your whole job is triaging an endless queue of pull requests.
    Approve clean diffs to keep features shipping, but catch the risky ones before they
    explode in production — and before your manager loses patience.
  
  Balance three competing meters to keep your job:
  
    🧯 Production Stability – Too many bugs in prod and things start catching fire.
    🚀 Delivery Velocity – Say “no” too often and you’ll be blamed for blocking the roadmap.
    📊 Manager Satisfaction – Walk the tightrope between speed and safety… or get fired.
  
  
    Each in-game day brings new rules and trickier PRs: security crackdowns, performance scares,
    missing tests, rushed hotfixes, and more. Read the diff, spot the bug patterns, and decide:
    Approve or Request changes?
  
  Features
  
    Papers Please–style judgment gameplay, but for GitHub pull requests.
    Short workday loops (9–17h) with building pressure and PR waves.
    Three meters to manage: stability, velocity, and job security.
    Choose your preferred stack flavor: TypeScript, Python, Java, or Rust.
    Playable in the browser, no install required.
    💾 Open source – check out the code and contribute on GitHub:
      
        https://github.com/llamojha/approve-please
      
    
  
  Languages
  
    🌐 Available in English and Spanish / Español.

moreInfo.Author: amllamojha
moreInfo.Tags: GitHub, Open Source, Working Simulator

game.title: Casserole Quest
game.short_text: Collect all the recipes to become a microwave master chef!

details.title: Casserole Quest
details.description:
Head back to the 70s in an era where the microwave has been gaining popularity. Cookbooks are being released left and right and your goal is to create a compendium of all the cookbooks combined to become the Microwave Master Chef!
The goal of this game is to combine ingredients in the microwave to create new recipes to add to your cookbook. See if you can climb the tiers to combine all the base ingredients into one final dish and eat the Last Supper!
Input (web)
Click and drag
Space = Pause
Hover on cards for tips and ingredient lists
Input (mobile)
Press and drag
Long press anywhere other than cards = Pause

MICROWAVE MOISTURE DRAIN
The microwave has its own personal drain rate that grows as you create.
Ingredient cards have 3 stats that influence the total drain rate:
Water - Moisture content of the ingredients 
Heat Resistance -  The ingredient's resistance to the microwave's moisture drain
Volatility - Multiplier added to the microwave's moisture drain.
Your ingredients must survive 15 seconds in the microwave without losing all their combined moisture. When successful, a new recipe will be created and added to your recipe box.
Use the free samples in the fridge to replenish your ingredient stock and get your ingredients certified organic to upgrade their stats!

Created by CircuitsGames in Godot for GitHub Game Off 2025.


Special thanks to 3dSquare for the background music!

Wish I could do more but I'm out of time!

game.title: ScillyScope
game.short_text: Decode a haiku hidden in sound waves

details.title: ScillyScope
details.description:
ScillyScope
My entry for GitHub's Game Off 2025
How good is your ear? Can you hear a note and know which frequency it is? What if I help you out by showing it to you on an oscilloscope?
Ok, so assigning the letters of the alphabet to a keyboard and using it to encode the words of a haiku doesn't make for the most melodious tune, but I challenge you to listen carefully and reproduce the notes of each word to uncover my silly poem about WAVES.
How to play
Controls: mouse or touch
Play the hidden haiku, then you'll be able to select one hidden word at a time. Use the piano keyboard to record the notes you hear for the selected word (don't worry, timing doesn't matter, just find the right frequencies.) Each frequency is mapped to a letter of the alphabet - play the right notes to spell out the word. Once you think you've got it, press play to see if you're right. You pick which word to decode next, so start with the easy/shorter ones first!
About
I created this game solo, using only html, css, and javascript, with the help of Copilot to "speed up the process" (if you know you know.) This is the first time I've used the Web Audio API and am finding that it's not very consistent across browsers, so if you leave any comments about the sound (good or bad) please let me know which browser and platform you're using.
You can find the source code at https://github.com/sarahRosannaBusch/ScillyScope

game.title: Sineonara
game.short_text: Submission for Game Off 2025

details.title: Sineonara
details.description:
You control a sine wave. Adjust its amplitude and frequency and balancing the charge required for each attack to prevent waves of shapes from reaching the left side of the screen.
There is no end goal; just try and hold out for as long as possible. You regain some health at the start of each wave.
My (riSygneD's) submission for Game Off 2025. I only found out about this about a week or so before the deadline and figured it would be fun, and I was right! My best is reaching Total 110, though I don't doubt I could do better with more time :)

game.title: Gravity Surfer
game.short_text: Navigate the gravitational waves of space using the gravitational pull of other planets to travel to the next wormhole.

details.title: Gravity Surfer
details.description:
Navigate space and  intergalactic travel using the waves of gravity produced by a bodies bending of spacetime. This is a simple puzzle platformer which shouldn't take very long to complete. This game is a submission for the 2025 Game OFF game jam hosted by Github. This is still a work in progress and I intend to eventually release a much more completed version on steam with new obstacles a storyline and more.
Please let me know any and all feedback you have in the comments, your suggestions will likely make it into the final product. 
Most importantly enjoy this short demo and comment the attempts it takes for you to finish it!! 
Note: The final level is hard but is 100% completable.How To Play:

Hit the wormhole to get to the next level
Use the left and right arrows to aim.
Use the up and down arrows to adjust power
Press SPACE to fire
use tab or esc to pause. (tab if in maximized mode on webpage)
Music:
"Cosmic Lights" by Chance de la SoulSource: https://opengameart.org/content/cosmic-lights
Licensehttps://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/

details.title: Graveyard Shifts
details.description:
༼ つ ╹ ╹ ༽つ
Those ghosts are toast!
Naughty ghosts are tormenting the good ghosts of the graveyard and you've been hired to send them to the afterlife. Using the "Toast Ghost 666," try different wave combos to get the "Wanted" ghost types into the center line and hit "Capture." But be careful! If you capture any non-wanted ghosts you'll lose reputation points. If you lose too many reputation points, or if any Wanted ghosts escape, you lose.
. ݁₊ ⊹ . ݁ ⟡ ݁ . ⊹ ₊ ݁.
Controls (mouse)

👻 Click the different white wave buttons to turn them on. You can have multiple buttons on at once!
👻 Click the invert switch to run the wave inverted (which does its opposite).
👻 Click the dial to change how many spaces the affected ghosts will move.
👻 Click the "Run" lever to launch the waves. Keep an eye on the amount of Charges you have: you can only fire off the machine that many times before you lose!
. ݁₊ ⊹ . ݁ ⟡ ݁ . ⊹ ₊ ݁.
Tips

👻 Keep track with a phone or notepad of which ghosts or hats are affected by which waves!
👻 You also gain a reputation point for each Wanted ghost you capture. Sometimes it's worth it to capture good ghosts, even with the reputation lost, to save on charges
. ݁₊ ⊹ . ݁ ⟡ ݁ . ⊹ ₊ ݁.
Credits
Made for Github Game Off 2025. Theme: Waves Source on Github Game by Kenny Murphey (ChefPancake) and Em Handy Programing/Level Design: Kenny Murphey (ChefPancake) using Bevy EngineGraphics/Art Direction: Em Handy Soundtrack: SuloSoundsSounds: Made using Bosca Ceoil Blue

game.title: Pray to Lucifer demo
game.short_text: Fight your way out by using light for killing waves of monsters, who can't stand the brightness of your palm

details.title: Pray to Lucifer demo
details.description:
This is a GameOff 2025 jam entry. The theme of the jam was "waves". We implemented them in two different ways. Can you find them?Pray to Lucifer is a top-down single-player wave-shooter game with elements of tower defense, where the player represents a savior who carries “the light” to liberate the land from darkness. His goal is to kill all shadow enemies to liberate the land. In his quest, he will build and make use of ‘densoul’ (mirrors, lenses, …), which apply various effects to the beam and its trajectory. It blends wave-shooting and monster enemies similar to Vampire Survivor, with true physics behind optics.
